deck_title: Determine Area of Triangles Lesson launch
lesson_topic: Determine Area of Triangles Lesson launch

learning_targets: I can determine the area of a triangle using a related rectangle or parallelogram.
success_criteria: I can identify the base and height.
I can explain why the triangle area formula works.
I can solve a source problem and justify my strategy.

sessions.session_title: Determine Area of Triangles Lesson launch

lines: Estimate which shape has the greater area.
opening_source.source_excerpt: Lesson launch What shapes do you notice? Estimate which shape has the greater area.

lines: How does the rectangle help you reason about triangle area?
modeling_source.source_excerpt: Rectangle area review How does the rectangle help you reason about triangle area?

lines: Explain why doubling the triangle creates a parallelogram.
What misconception should we watch for?
collaborative_source.source_excerpt: Explain why doubling the triangle creates a parallelogram. What misconception should we watch for?

lines: Triangle A has base 10 units and height 6 units. What is its area?
guided_source.source_excerpt: Triangle A has base 10 units and height 6 units. What is its area? Which measurement is the height?

lines: Find the area of Triangle B with base 12 units and height 9 units.
Show your work and label units.
independent_source.source_excerpt: Find the area of Triangle B with base 12 units and height 9 units. Show your work and label units.

lines: A triangle has area 24 square units and height 6 units. What is the base?
closure_source.source_excerpt: A triangle has area 24 square units and height 6 units. What is the base? Explain your reasoning.
